title: A Christmas Carol in Prose; Being a Ghost Story of Christmas
coverScene: An old man sits by a small fire in a dimly lit room with a Christmas tree in the corner, surrounded by snow-covered windows.

sentences.text: Scrooge was a man with a cold heart.
He was also a very hard man, who was known by many in the town.
He did not like the holiday of Christmas at all.
Many people were afraid of him.
They knew he was not a kind person.
His face often held a serious and firm look.
He kept to himself most of the time.
This was how his life was lived.
If Scrooge had been more open, he might have had many friends.
Scrooge kept a small fire in his office space.
The office was always kept cold.
His employee, Bob Cratchit, had an even smaller fire.
The air in the room felt very cold.
It was very cold outside the building, which was old.
Snow fell from the sky with no end.
The wind made a sad sound around the windows.
Scrooge did not seem to care about the low temperature.
His employee shook from the cold as he worked.
Scrooge’s family member came to visit him.
This was Fred, his sister’s son, who was a kind man.
Fred said, 'Happy Christmas' to Scrooge.
Scrooge did not like these words, which were full of joy.
He did not like the holiday at all.
His family member tried to be kind and warm.
If Fred had not come, Scrooge would have been completely alone.
Scrooge believed Christmas was a waste of time.
He thought it took people away from their important work.
He did not want to celebrate it.
He just wanted to work and earn money.
He did not understand joy or good will.
If he had understood joy, his life would be different.
His days were filled only with his business.
Scrooge’s family member tried to make him feel happy.
He spoke of the good things about Christmas.
But Scrooge would not change his mind.
He was too cold and too hard in his ways of thinking.
He sent his family member away from the office.
The kind words were not heard by Scrooge.
The weather outside was truly very bad.
There was much snow on the ground everywhere.
The air was very cold, a bitter cold.
But Scrooge did not care about the cold or the snow.
He only cared about his own work and profit.
No warm feeling was offered by him.
He just wanted to be left alone and sleep at home.
But Scrooge’s life was about to change.
Something strange was going to happen soon to him.
It would change Scrooge’s life forever.
A series of important events would begin this night.
He would never be the same after these events.
This would be a big and lasting change.
He was not prepared for what was coming.
If Scrooge had been a kind man, he would have been happy.
But he was not kind at all to people.
So, he was not happy in his life.
He was alone on the night of Christmas, as always.
He often felt a great sad feeling deep inside.
His heart was closed to the world.
His employee, Bob Cratchit, was also very cold.
Bob had a small fire for his work space, which was not much.
But it was not enough heat for the long day.
He wished Scrooge would give him more coal for the fire.
He knew Scrooge had much coal in his own room.
But Scrooge would not share his fire.
If more coal had been given, Bob would have been warm.
If Scrooge’s employee had more coal, he would have been warm.
But he did not have more coal to use.
So, he was very cold all day long.
He sat at his desk, shaking from the cold.
His hands were blue from the low temperature.
His work was done with great difficulty.
Scrooge’s family member came to visit him again.
This was later in the day, as evening came.
He brought some food and drink to share.
He tried to make Scrooge happy with his visit.
But Scrooge still had a difficult mood.
He was not pleased by the kind offer.
He did not welcome the food or the company.
If Scrooge had eaten the food, he might have felt better.
But he did not eat the food, which was warm.
So, he still felt bad inside himself.
He just sat there, still and cold.
He looked at his family member with a cold eye.
His family member tried again to talk to him, but Scrooge pushed him away.
Scrooge’s life was truly not good for him.
He felt cold inside himself, in his own heart.
He often felt alone and very sad.
He did not have any friends at all.
If he had been a kind person, he would have had friends.
But he was not kind, so he was alone on the night of Christmas.
Something strange was going to happen to Scrooge.
It would change his life forever, in a big way.
He would never be the same again, it was clear.
This change would start tonight, after dark.
He was not prepared for it at all.
His future was about to be changed forever.
Scrooge would meet a strange visitor.
This visitor, which was not of this world, would appear.
This visitor would show him something new and important.
It would make Scrooge change his ways and actions.
This change would be great and much needed.
It would affect everything in his life.
A message would be clearly sent to him.
If Scrooge had not met the strange visitor, he would still be difficult.
He would still feel bad and keep his cold heart.
But he did meet the visitor, which was a surprise.
So, he changed his life, day by day.
He became a kind and happy man.
This was a good change for all.
Scrooge’s employee was happy when Scrooge changed.
He was glad that Scrooge was kind to him.
He felt happy to work for Scrooge now, which was new.
His work life was much better and warmer.
More coal was given to his fire each day.
If Scrooge had not changed, his employee would not have been happy.
Scrooge’s family member was also happy when Scrooge changed.
He was glad that Scrooge was kind and open.
He felt happy to visit Scrooge, which was a joy.
Their family connection grew strong and real.
This made Fred very pleased.
If Scrooge had not changed, his family member would not have been happy.
Scrooge was happy when he changed his life.
He felt good about himself and his new actions.
He was glad to be kind to other people now.
A great weight was lifted from his heart.
He saw the world with new eyes and understanding.
If Scrooge had not changed, he would not have been happy.
Scrooge’s life was good after he changed his ways.
He was kind to everyone around him.
He was happy in his heart and in his home.
He had friends and family who loved him very much.
He shared his money and his time with others.
This was a new way of being, full of light.
If Scrooge had not changed, his life would not have been good.
It would have been a poor and cold life.
But he did change, which was wonderful.
So, his life was good and full of meaning.
He was glad that he had changed his ways.
This made all the difference to him and to others.

What was the weather like outside Scrooge's office?
Who came to visit Scrooge in his office?

sentences.text: Scrooge was a cold man.
He was a hard man.
He never felt love.
He liked no one.
Scrooge was busy with money.
Money was very important.
He worked all day.
He counted money daily.
He did not like Christmas.
He disliked happy times.
Many knew Scrooge.
They were often afraid.
He was not a good friend.
He had no friends.
His face was serious.
He did not often smile.
He did not spend money.
He was careful with each piece of money.
He kept his money close.
He thought only of himself.
Scrooge worked in a big office.
It was a dark, old place.
He had a small fire.
This fire kept him warm.
It was not very big.
It gave little heat.
His worker, Bob Cratchit, worked near him.
Bob was a kind, good man.
Bob had a desk in the room.
He also had a fire.
Bob's fire was smaller.
It gave very little heat.
Bob was always cold.
He put on more clothes.
He tried to keep warm.
His job was hard.
Outside, the weather was very cold.
It was Christmas night.
The air was cold.
People walked fast.
They wanted to go home.
They wanted to be warm.
The city had much snow.
Then, Scrooge's young family member came.
His name was Fred.
Fred was a happy young man.
He had a big smile.
He came through the door.
He looked at Scrooge with a good feeling.
"Merry Christmas, Uncle!" Fred said with a loud voice.
Scrooge looked at him coldly.
He did not like these words.
"Christmas? Nonsense!" Scrooge said.
"What is Christmas?"
"It is a waste of time!"
Fred felt a little sad.
But he kept smiling.
"Christmas is a good day, Uncle," Fred said.
"It is a time for family.
It is a time for love.
We should be happy today."
Scrooge shook his head.
He did not believe Fred.
Scrooge thought Christmas was a waste.
He thought it was not good.
"You lose money on Christmas," Scrooge said.
"You do not work.
People spend money.
They do not earn money.
If people do not work, they will not earn.
This is bad for business."
Fred tried to talk more.
He wanted Scrooge to understand.
"But Christmas brings a good feeling, Uncle," Fred said.
"It makes people happy.
It is a good feeling.
Many love this time of year."
Scrooge did not listen.
He turned his face away.
He just wanted to work.
He wanted to make money.
He did not want holidays.
Holidays were not good for business.
He had never liked them.
He would never like them.
Fred still tried to make Scrooge happy.
He spoke about Christmas dinner.
"Come to our house tonight, Uncle," Fred said.
"My wife will be there.
We will have a good time.
Please come."
Scrooge looked at him coldly.
"No, thank you," he said.
"You are poor.
Why are you happy?
I do not understand."
Fred said, "I am happy because of Christmas, Uncle.
It makes my heart light."
Scrooge did not understand this.
He thought Fred was strange.
He would not change his mind.
He would not go to dinner.
He was too cold inside.
His heart was too hard for a good feeling.
Fred saw it was no use.
He said he must leave his family member.
He left the office slowly.
Scrooge was alone again.
The weather outside grew worse.
It was very bad.
More snow began to fall.
The wind was strong and cold.
The sky was dark.
City lights began to give light.
People walked fast through streets.
They pulled coats close.
They wanted to leave the cold.
They looked for warm places.
But Scrooge did not care.
He stayed in his office.
He did not feel cold.
Or he did not want to feel it.
He worked on his papers.
He counted his money.
He thought only of business.
Nothing else was important.
He did not look out.
Scrooge wanted to be alone.
He wanted no one to talk.
He did not like noise.
He disliked people talking around him.
He just wanted quiet.
He wanted to work in peace.
After work, he would go home.
He would eat a small dinner alone.
Then he would go to sleep.
He did this every day.
He had no friends to visit.
He had no family to see.
His life was simple.
It was always the same.
He thought this was a good life.
A life with no trouble.
He did not need other people.
He did not need their help.
He was fine alone.
But this Christmas night was different.
Scrooge's life would change.
He did not know this yet.
Something strange was coming.
It would happen very soon.
It would happen in his old house.
This thing would change Scrooge.
It would change his mind.
It would change his heart.
His life would never be the same.
It would change him for a long time.
He would learn many things.
He would see the past.
He would see the future.
Scrooge would meet visitors soon.
They would show a new way to live.
His life would become better.

quiz.q: How did Scrooge feel about Christmas?
options: He thought Christmas was a waste of time.
He did not know what Christmas was.

quiz.q: What was the weather like outside Scrooge's office?
options: It was snowing and very cold.

quiz.q: What did Scrooge's nephew, Fred, ask him to do?
options: To come to his house for Christmas dinner.
